message:
fix(health): properly kill timed-out CLI processes and prevent unhandled rejections

The health check endpoint spawns CLI processes to verify tool availability.
Previously, when a timeout occurred, the process was left running and the
resultPromise could reject after the timeout handler had already thrown,
causing potential unhandled promise rejections in Node.js.

Changes:
- Add `timedOut` flag to track timeout state
- Kill the spawned process immediately when timeout fires (proc.kill())
- Wrap kill() in try/catch since process may have already exited
- Catch and suppress resultPromise rejection if timeout already occurred
  to prevent unhandled rejection warnings

This ensures clean resource cleanup during CLI health checks and prevents
spurious error logs in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
